title: T-052 — ccp_deviation_escalation_v1 DSL rule registration (09-e-03)
prompt:
# T-052 — ccp_deviation_escalation_v1 DSL rule registration

PRD: §10.1 rule registry, §10.2 rule definition (verbatim JSON), §11.5 V-QA-CCP-003, §7.1 09-e-03
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Register ccp_deviation_escalation_v1 DSL rule in rule_definitions per §10.2 verbatim JSON. Trigger on haccp_monitoring_records.after_insert_or_update; on within_limits=false AND deviation_threshold_seconds exceeded → auto-create NCR via T-041 + notify hygiene_lead/quality_lead/prod_manager + auto-hold WO if severity=critical.

## Implementation contract
1. Migration 0144 emits INSERT INTO rule_definitions per §10.2 verbatim JSON (rule_code='ccp_deviation_escalation_v1', tier='L1', trigger, condition with within_limits=false + threshold_seconds, actions: auto_create_ncr with severity_map, notify, auto_hold conditional on severity=critical).
2. apps/web/src/server/quality/ccp/ruleHandler.ts — DSL rule action handlers: auto_create_ncr invokes T-041 autoCreate.ts; notify writes notification rows; auto_hold creates a quality_holds row (T-007 hold create Server Action with reference_type='wo').
3. Hookup into monitorCcpTick (T-051) so the rule engine evaluates after insert.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0144_ccp_deviation_escalation_v1.sql [create]
- apps/web/src/server/quality/ccp/ruleHandler.ts [create]
- apps/web/src/server/quality/ccp/__tests__/ruleHandler.test.ts [create]
- apps/web/src/server/quality/haccp/actions.ts [modify]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0144 applied, when SELECT * FROM rule_definitions WHERE rule_code='ccp_deviation_escalation_v1' runs, then 1 row exists with tier='L1' and definition JSON verbatim per §10.2
2. Given a haccp_monitoring_records insert with within_limits=false AND ccp.deviation_threshold_seconds=0 AND ccp.hazard_type='biological', when the rule fires, then T-041 autoCreate is called with severity='critical', a quality.ncr.created event is emitted, and a quality_holds row is created via the auto_hold action with reason='ccp_deviation_escalation' priority='critical'
3. Given the same insert but ccp.hazard_type='chemical', when the rule fires, then NCR severity='major' per the rule severity_map and auto_hold is NOT triggered (severity != critical)
4. Given the rule_definitions row exists, when rule-simulator runs against the last 90d of haccp_monitoring_records (audit/rule-testing pattern per §10.4), then it reports zero false positives vs current behavior (records that today would not have escalated should still not escalate)

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: ccp/__tests__/ruleHandler.test.ts asserting all 4 ACs (rule row insert, biological=critical+hold, chemical=major no-hold, rule-simulator zero-FP) before code
- Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter web test ccp/ruleHandler'

## Risk red lines
- Do not deviate from §10.2 verbatim JSON (tier, trigger, severity_map, actions)
- Do not bypass T-041 autoCreate — single auto-create path enforced (translation-notes §M-08)
- Do not auto-hold for non-critical (severity_map drives auto_hold conditional)
- Rule registration must use rule_definitions table (02-SETTINGS §7) and be read-only at runtime per §10.4

pipeline_inputs.description: Register ccp_deviation_escalation_v1 DSL rule + implement rule action handlers (auto_create_ncr, notify, auto_hold) + wire into monitorCcpTick.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0144 inserts the rule_definitions row per §10.2 verbatim JSON: tier='L1', trigger='haccp_monitoring_records.after_insert_or_update', condition checks within_limits=false AND ccp.deviation_threshold_seconds <= seconds_since_last_in_limits_reading, actions auto_create_ncr (with severity_map biological/allergen→critical, chemical/physical→major, default→minor), notify (hygiene_lead/quality_lead/prod_manager + in_app + email_if_critical), auto_hold conditional on severity=critical. ruleHandler.ts action handlers call T-041 autoCreate for NCR, write notification rows, and call hold create Server Action for auto_hold. monitorCcpTick (T-051) invokes the rule engine after the GENERATED within_limits is computed. PRD anchors verified: §10.1 rule registry (3-row table); §10.2 verbatim JSON (lines 1382-1426); §11.5 V-QA-CCP-003 (rule auto-creates NCR); §7.1 09-e-03 scope.
out_of_scope: Do not implement NCR autoCreate.ts (T-041 owner)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0144 applied, when SELECT FROM rule_definitions WHERE rule_code='ccp_deviation_escalation_v1' runs, then 1 row exists with tier='L1' and definition JSON matching §10.2 verbatim

Given a within_limits=false insert on a biological CCP, when the rule fires, then T-041 autoCreate is called with severity='critical', quality.ncr.created event emits, and an auto_hold quality_holds row is created with reason='ccp_deviation_escalation' priority='critical'
Given a within_limits=false insert on a chemical CCP, when the rule fires, then severity='major' and auto_hold is NOT triggered

Given the rule registered, when rule-simulator runs against 90d historic monitoring records (§10.4 pattern), then zero false positives are reported (no record that today would not escalate, escalates under the new rule)
test_strategy: RED: ruleHandler.test.ts asserting all 4 ACs (rule row, biological→critical+hold, chemical→major no-hold, simulator 0-FP) before code
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter web test ccp/ruleHandler'
risk_red_lines: Do not deviate from §10.2 verbatim JSON (tier, trigger, severity_map, actions)
Do not bypass T-041 autoCreate for NCR creation (single audit/outbox path)
Do not auto-hold non-critical severity (severity_map drives auto_hold conditional)
Use rule_definitions table from 02-SETTINGS §7 (read-only at runtime per §10.4)
